{{Quote|Stellar Web? Nonsense. That's an Imperial craft.|[[Warlord/Legends|Warlord]] [[Zsinj]], underestimating the guile of his enemies, both [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] and [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]|<ref name="X-Wing: Solo Command">''[[X-Wing: Solo Command]]''</ref>}}
The '''''Stellar Web''''' was an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Immobilizer 418 Interdictor-class Heavy Cruiser|Immobilizer 418 cruiser]] commanded by [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Barr Moutil]].

The ship was assigned to Imperial [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Rogriss]] as part of his fleet to destroy [[Warlord/Legends|Warlord]] [[Zsinj]]'s [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought]], [[Iron Fist (Executor-class)|''Iron Fist'']].

After contacting [[General/Legends|General]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], his [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] counterpart, in [[7 ABY/Legends|7.5 ABY]], Rogriss offered his enemies ''Stellar Web'' in return for a signed [[Garik Loran]] film and an opportunity to destroy Zsinj, despite the consequences for both military leaders if their collaboration were discovered. They decided the cruiser would be isolated from New Republic forces and protected by two of Solo's own [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]], ''[[Crynyd]]'' and [[Skyhook (Star Destroyer)|''Skyhook'']], to avoid suspicions from the ''Stellar Web''{{'s}} crew.

Under the personal command of Rogriss, ''Stellar Web'' succeeded in trapping ''Iron Fist'' in its [[Interdiction field/Legends|interdiction field]] at [[Vahaba]], but following an attempted suicide attack by a Zsinj [[Victory-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory''-class Star Destroyer]], ''[[Serpent's Smile]]'', the Interdictor was forced to break off. This allowed ''Iron Fist'' enough time to break free of the field and escape into [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]].

Following the Star Dreadnought's escape, ''Stellar Web'' departed. Solo wished Rogriss "good luck, with the warlord at least."
